bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with their worst possible offer (all valueF), giving me utility of 0.4×0.2 + 0.3×0.2 + 0.2×0.2 + 0.1×0.2 = 0.2. Their aggressive message is pure cheap talk — it's t=0.00, so there's no time pressure at all. I should counter with my ideal offer (all valueA) to maximize my utility of 1.0. I'll hold firm early and concede gradually as time progresses.

We're very early in the negotiation (t=0.02). The opponent is repeating their opening bid of all valueF (worst for me) while using aggressive rhetoric. Their messages are classic intimidation tactics ("I'll walk") which I should treat as cheap talk. Their behavior shows no concession whatsoever. I should hold firm near my ideal bid since we have plenty of time. My top utility bid is all valueA (utility = 1.0). I'll stay with that or very close to it.

Their offers give me very low utility (all valueF = 0.20 each, or nearly that). My best bid gives me utility of 1.0. There's no reason to concede significantly this early — I should maintain a near-maximum bid. I'll keep my top bid since we're only at 4% of negotiation time.
